danger_signals.visual_form: grouped units on hexagonal tiles in isometric view
danger_signals.meaning: concentrated military threat; up to 8 units per tile indicate organized hostile force

danger_signals.visual_form: race-specific unit sprites and city visual markers
danger_signals.meaning: alignment-based hostility; good races show danger near evil factions; visual race distinction signals threat level

danger_signals.visual_form: unit behavioral changes and grouping restrictions
danger_signals.meaning: forced hostile race units in same group show lower morale; visual/behavioral degradation signals instability

danger_signals.visual_form: city visual state changes under weak occupation
danger_signals.meaning: conquered orc/goblin cities without strong military presence show revolt indicators; visual garrison strength communicates control

safety_signals.visual_form: friendly city visual markers and allied unit sprites
safety_signals.meaning: cities of same race naturally friendly; visual race identity signals sanctuary and recruitment capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: military unit density in city tiles
safety_signals.meaning: multiple stacked units in city area indicate oppressive control; prevents revolt and signals security

safety_signals.visual_form: city visual tier progression and visible structures
safety_signals.meaning: upgraded cities show advanced unit production capability; visual enhancement signals stability and military capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: shared diplomatic status and race relation indicators
safety_signals.meaning: allied race units cooperate without morale penalty; visual grouping shows peaceful coexistence

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: visually hostile to evil-aligned races; unit sprites reflect good alignment
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: elven cities have distinctive architecture; race-specific structures in controlled territory

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: naturally hostile to elves; evil-aligned units; resistance to elf rule visible in revolt mechanics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: orc cities show tribal/crude architecture; conquered orc settlements require heavy occupation visuals

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: evil alignment creates visual hostility markers with good races
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: dark-themed city visuals; underground or shadowy settlement indicators

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: evil-aligned; necromantic unit visual themes; incompatible with living races
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: undead settlements show decay/death visuals; corrupted landscape around undead territories

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: neutral to good alignment; distinct from evil races
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: underground/mountain settlement visuals; stone and metal-themed architecture

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: chaotic evil alignment; visually distinct hostile indicators
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: goblin cities show chaotic/ramshackle architecture; conquered goblin territories need strong visual control

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: reptilian unit visual theme; alignment-dependent hostility

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: human cities show standard medieval architecture; cultural visual diversity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: friendly city sprites; allied unit cooperation in stacks without morale penalty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: independent units of allied race can be hired; cities show prosperity indicators; units stand together peacefully

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: hostile unit sprites; visual indicators of enmity; city appearance reflects cultural conflict
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: units of hostile races refuse to group effectively; lower morale indicators on mixed stacks; desertion risk visible in unit behavior

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: city visual changes to occupier's faction; garrison unit presence becomes visible on multiple city tiles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: oppressive occupation prevents revolts; visual military dominance displayed; conquered city may show visual damage or corruption

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: city shows signs of unrest; minimal garrison visuals; potentially unstable settlement appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: conquered hostile cities at risk of revolt; visual indicators of civil unrest; unit morale degradation signals

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: shared visual indicators between allied factions; cities may show combined architectural markers; units display non-hostile stance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: allied units cooperate without penalty; faction-specific goodwill spells modify visual race relation markers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: altar activation creates large-scale visual spell effects across map region; magical aura displays across affected territories
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: global race relation spells visually cascade across faction territories; widespread behavioral change in unit morale/hostility

environmental_storytelling.example: elven cities show refined crystalline/natural structures; orc settlements display crude tribal construction; undead zones show decay and corruption visuals
conquered hostile city displays heavy garrison presence on multiple tiles; visual infrastructure shows domination through military density

environmental_storytelling.visual_example: good-aligned unit groups contrast visually with evil-aligned factions; visual sprite differences communicate moral framework

environmental_storytelling.example: upgraded cities show visual tier advancement; enhanced structures signal military/economic capacity; visual prosperity indicates faction strength
wandering unit groups on map tiles show faction affiliation through visual unit appearance; neutral groups have distinct sprite markers
magical altars visually anchor territories; their control and visual dominance signal magical power concentration; mana accumulation shown through visual aura states

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: morale degradation visible through unit animations/positioning; desertion risk indicated by unstable grouping
cohesive formation; stable morale; animation shows cooperation and combat readiness
visual indicators of experience gain; medal display (silver/gold) shows combat history; spell casting visual feedback
revolt risk without oppressive garrison; visual unrest indicators; potential visual damage or cultural markers of occupation
stable economic visual indicators; unit recruitment animations active; prosperity markers visible
can be hired through visual recruitment interaction; unit animations show readiness to serve
visual mana charge indicator; glowing aura intensifies as 50 mana per turn accumulates; ready-to-fire state shows through visual readiness

notes: Age of Wonders uses hexagonal isometric grid as primary world-state communication layer. Race alignment (good/evil/neutral) drives most visual distinction and behavioral compatibility signals. City control and occupation strength communicated through military unit density on map tiles rather than explicit garrison UI. Conquered city stability depends on visible occupying force—weak visual garrison presence indicates revolt risk. Hero progression shows through medal display (mechanical manifestation of combat experience). Diplomatic relations and race compatibility create cascading visual effects: hostile race pairings show morale degradation through unit positioning/animation. Alliance state removes these penalties and shows through unified visual stances. Spells with global effects create world-state shifts visible across entire faction territories.
